title: I Read My Sentence Steadily,
content:
I read my sentence steadily,
    Reviewed it with my eyes,
    To see that I made no mistake
    In its extremest clause, --

    The date, and manner of the shame;
    And then the pious form
    That "God have mercy" on the soul
    The jury voted him.

    I made my soul familiar
    With her extremity,
    That at the last it should not be
    A novel agony,

    But she and Death, acquainted,
    Meet tranquilly as friends,
    Salute and pass without a hint --
    And there the matter ends.
meaning:
"I Read My Sentence Steadily," is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
